GAP-LAU-01 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | R-LAU | Laundry room specification gaps: (1) drum opening height 380–915 mm AFF — only ADA S611 provides quantified spec; all other jurisdictions silent on appliance height; (2) clear floor 762×1219 mm parallel to appliance; (3) turning radius 1500mm shared/1300mm residential confirmed in SV/DE/AU/US; (4) main-floor location requirement (basement = categorical exclusion). Guidebook R-LAU item requires spec expansion | 2026-03-18 17:00
GAP-LAU-02 | P3 | OPEN | multilingual-research | R-LAU | NDIS SDA High Physical Support specifies laundry appliances accessible from seated/standing with adjustable height — this is the most advanced jurisdiction-specific provision; consider as best-practice tier for R-LAU | 2026-03-18 17:00
GAP-A09-01 | P1 | CLOSED-DELETED | multilingual-research | A-09 | CRITICAL: A-09 cites 0.1 m/s RMS as vibration isolation threshold for disability populations. This value does not correspond to any located ISO 2631-1, ISO 10137, or BS 6472 threshold. ISO 2631-1 uses weighted acceleration (m/s²); 0.1 m/s velocity may be from DIN 4150-2 (structural vibration, Germany) but its application to built-environment disability design is unverified. Garcia-Mendez 2013 confirms WC users exceed ISO 2631-1 health caution zone in daily use. No disability-specific floor vibration standard exists in any of 14 languages searched. SOURCE OF 0.1 m/s THRESHOLD MUST BE VERIFIED BEFORE PUBLICATION | 2026-03-18 17:00
GAP-A09-02 | P2 | CLOSED-NOTED | multilingual-research | A-09 | ISO 2631-1 was explicitly NOT designed for SCI/disability populations (Chénier & Aissaoui 2014) — any citation of ISO 2631-1 in A-09 must carry this caveat; populations for whom A-09 is most relevant (MOB/SCI) are precisely those for whom the standard is least validated | 2026-03-18 17:00
REVIEW-LAU-A09-01 | REVIEW | PARTIAL | workplan-orchestrator | A-09 + R-LAU | 2 escalation signals: A-09 has an unverified threshold cited as normative (P1); R-LAU lacks jurisdictional breadth for spec confidence. Recommend: (1) citation-verifier targeted run on A-09 0.1 m/s source; (2) item-specification-writer pass on R-LAU with ADA S611 as primary reference | 2026-03-18 17:00

GAP-RAP-01 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | A-01/A-02/A-08 | NDV/AUT acoustic provisions: no jurisdiction has a quantified RT60 target specific to autistic users. Bettarello et al. (2021) and Caniato et al. (2024) are the strongest available evidence but do not produce a normative standard. Guidebook's current NDV acoustic targets (NC-25, RT60 ≤ 0.6 s) are derived from general standards and are confirmed insufficient for this population. Recommend flagging all Category A items with NDV population coding as THIN-POPULATION-SPEC pending new research. | 2026-03-19

GAP-RAP-02 | P1 | CLOSED [2026-03-19] | citation-verifier | A-01/A-02/A-03 etc. | Bettarello et al. (2021) Applied Sciences 11(9), 3942 DOI:10.3390/app11093942 — VERIFIED CORRECT. No citation correction required. | 2026-03-19

GAP-RAP-03 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | A-08 | NC-25 to dB(A) equivalence note absent from guidebook. NC-25 ≈ 33–37 dB(A) depending on frequency content. DIN 18041:2016 does not use NC nomenclature but aligns. Cross-reference equivalence should be added to A-08 evidence basis note. | 2026-03-19

GAP-RAP-04 | P3 | OPEN | bibliography-updater | Appendix A / bibliography | AIJ AIJES-S001-2008 (学校施設の音環境保全基準) — primary Japanese room acoustic standard for schools — absent from guidebook bibliography and Appendix A. Add before next publication. | 2026-03-19

GAP-RAP-05 | P3 | OPEN | bibliography-updater | Appendix A / bibliography | UNI 11532-2:2020 — primary Italian school acoustic standard; explicitly addresses hearing-deficit and attention/concentration disorder populations (categoria A3.1/A4); absent from guidebook bibliography and Appendix A. Add before next publication. | 2026-03-19

GAP-RAP-06 | P2 | OPEN | item-specification-writer | A-02/A-08 + DEAF item | DEAF-specific RT60 target: ANSI/ASA S12.60-2010/Part 1 specifies ≤ 0.3 s for classrooms with hearing-impaired students (≤ 283 m³) — Tier 1 evidence (Iglehart 2020). Guidebook does not state a DEAF-specific RT60 sub-target. Recommend adding a population-differentiated note to the NR-EDU matrix and to A-08 HVAC noise control item: where DEAF is a primary population in a classroom ≤ 283 m³, RT60 target is ≤ 0.3 s (not the general ≤ 0.5–0.6 s). | 2026-03-19

FILE: gap_register.md — APPEND

GAP-030 | P3 | OPEN | multilingual-research | visual-alerting-and-wayfinding-light|VIS,DEAF,DBL | KO: No dedicated visual alarm device (VAD/strobe) standard found in Korean welfare facility standard or building code. General hearing loop provision confirmed (복지시설 기준) but luminous intensity, flash rate, and coverage requirements for visual fire alarms absent. Flag for targeted KO pass. | 2026-03-19 13:30

GAP-031 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | visual-alerting-and-wayfinding-light|VIS,DEAF,DBL | JA: 光警報装置 (visual alarm device) is recommended but not legally mandatory under 消防法 (Fire Services Act). The 2016 FDMA guideline promotes installation at facilities serving hearing-impaired users but does not create a legal obligation. This is a below-best-practice gap relative to UK (CPD 2013 mandatory), EU (EN 54-23 mandatory for all new VADs), US (ADA mandatory in places of public accommodation), and FR (Code du travail mandatory in workplaces). JA guideline status should be noted in B-10 item evidence base as a jurisdiction-specific limitation. | 2026-03-19 13:30

GAP-032 | P3 | OPEN | multilingual-research | luminance-contrast-and-pattern|VIS,DEM,NDV | ZH, JA, KO: Dementia-specific floor pattern and luminance contrast guidance not found in any East Asian standard or national advisory document. Tactile indicator (盲道/点字ブロック/점자블록) contrast requirements confirmed, but DEM-specific plain flooring provision, inverse contrast rule (≤10 LRV at adjacent floor materials), and patterned flooring contraindication are absent from all three jurisdictions' standards and advisory documents. DSDC-equivalent national dementia design guidance does not appear to exist in published form for ZH, JA, or KO. Flag for targeted pass via MHLW (JA), MOHW (KR), and MCA (CN) dementia care facility design standards. | 2026-03-19 13:30

GAP-033 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | luminance-contrast-and-pattern|VIS,DEM,NDV | Thin empirical basis for LRV 30% threshold: only one confirmed OT primary empirical study (Lukman et al. 2020, HERD Journal, DOI: 10.1177/1064804619841841) directly validates the 30% LRV minimum for accessible tactile indicators. Lukman et al. found 30% adequate but users preferred higher contrast. All other jurisdictions adopt the threshold normatively (BS 8300, AS 1428.1, EN 17210, DIN 32975 K≥0.4 approximately equivalent) without citing independent empirical derivation. C-04 evidence base should carry THIN-EMPIRICAL disclosure: [Tier 3-6; one primary OT empirical study confirms adequacy; original derivation source not identified in literature search; March 2026]. Evidence review recommended at next revision. | 2026-03-19 13:30

GAP-034 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | therapeutic-lighting-design|ALL | Circadian lighting (EML/MEDI metric) absent from national building codes in all reviewed jurisdictions except via WELL v2 and ISO/CIE 8995-1:2025 (both voluntary/international). Only SV (Arbetsmiljöverket 2019: CS ≥0.3 for ≥30 min morning as workplace guidance) and EN (WELL v2, CIBSE TM12 circadian guidance) have any beyond-code specification. FI, FR, DE, ZH, JA, KO have natural light statutory requirements only — no circadian-effective metric in any national standard. B-01 specification (≥150 EML daytime) is currently ahead of all statutory standards globally and is grounded in WELL v2 voluntary standard only. Recommended action: (1) confirm ISO/CIE 8995-1:2025 publication status and whether it introduces a mandatory circadian metric; (2) cite Konis 2017 and the Solarlits 2024 systematic review as the primary evidence basis for the EML specification value. | 2026-03-19 13:30

GAP-035 | P3 | OPEN | multilingual-research | visual-alerting-and-wayfinding-light|VIS,DEAF,DBL | No jurisdiction has a mandatory quantified vibrotactile standard for sleeping areas (hotel guest rooms, residential care bedrooms, hospital patient rooms). Under-pillow/bed-shaker provision is universally recommended by RNID (UK), HLAA (US), and Bufdir (NO) but no standard specifies: device type, minimum vibration intensity (m/s² or equivalent), activation latency, or coverage obligation. B-10 and H-04 items should note this as a best-practice recommendation above any statutory basis, with disclosure: [Co-1/Tier 2 only; no statutory minimum in any jurisdiction; March 2026]. | 2026-03-19 13:30

# Gap Register Additions — Append to gap_register.md
# Produced: 2026-03-19 11:00
# Commit message: "session-consolidator: append GAP-ALS-01, GAP-ALS-02, GAP-SRS-01 [2026-03-19 11:00]"

GAP-ALS-01 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | A-10/A-11 | DEAF: Japan (JA) has no mandatory hearing loop provision in any building standard. The Barrier-Free Law (移動等円滑化法) and MLIT building standards do not mandate induction loop installation; all provision is voluntary, governed by municipal programmes (Kyoto, Kawasaki models) and the Zennancho hearing loop mark scheme. This is a significant jurisdiction gap relative to EU/UK/NO/FR mandatory standards and should be noted in A-10/A-11 jurisdiction coverage section. No remediation required to the specification itself; a jurisdiction note is the appropriate response. | 2026-03-19 11:00

GAP-ALS-02 | P3 | OPEN | multilingual-research | A-11 | DEAF: The French CEREMA standard (Arrêté 20 avril 2017 + CEREMA 2021) requires hearing loops (BIM — boucle à induction magnétique) in all ERP categories 1–4 AND in all assembly spaces ≥50 seats. This is more comprehensive than the guidebook's current A-11 trigger threshold (≥25 capacity), which is derived from the UK standard. CEREMA is already in the bibliography. The divergence is not currently noted at item level. Consider adding a cross-jurisdictional note in A-11 identifying CEREMA as a stricter best-practice reference for designers working in or to French standards. No change to the guidebook's own specification value (≥25) is required; this is a contextual note only. | 2026-03-19 11:00

GAP-SRS-01 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | A-16 | NDV/NEU/OFS/DEM: Individual occupant control of sensory inputs within the sensory room is currently listed as a secondary cross-reference (H-02) in A-16. Unwin, Powell & Jones (2022, PMC9340127; 2023) identified user control of sensory inputs as the single most important design variable in multi-sensory environments for autistic users — more important than static environmental parameters (fixed finishes, fixed lighting levels). This finding is Tier 3 (n=41; replicated 2023) and directly challenges the current A-16 specification balance. H-02 (Individual Environmental Control) should be elevated to PRIMARY cross-reference within A-16, with explicit statement that occupant-controlled dimming, sound management, and blackout are the primary design requirements; fixed environmental parameters (STC, RT60, NC) are the necessary enabling context. Additionally: Architecture & Access / Amaze (2025) identifies proximity to accessible sanitary facilities as a key location requirement not currently in A-16's location specification. Add to A-16 location section: "adjacent to accessible WC facility; accessible without passing through high-stimulation area." Both changes are item-specification-writer scope. | 2026-03-19 11:00

GAP-OT-BE-01 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | §VI.2 (Part VI OT role) | Young et al. (2019) scoping review finding has not changed substantially since 2019: no RCT or controlled evidence of outcome improvement from OT involvement on upstream design teams. The Russell protocol (2018) is still the only formalised OT-design process protocol. Evidence review recommended at v9.0. | 2026-03-19 14:30

GAP-DBL-BE-01 | P1 | OPEN | multilingual-research | §2.10 (DBL category) + Volume 2 DBL item specifications | No Tier 1 OT clinical research on DBL-specific built environment spatial provisions in any reviewed language. Current DBL specifications derive from Tier 2 (DbI guidelines, Sense UK) and Tier 4 (ISO 23599:2019) only. THIN-BASE disclosure should be added to all DBL item specifications in Volume 2 before v9.0 publication. | 2026-03-19 14:30

GAP-DBL-BE-02 | P2 | OPEN | multilingual-research | §2.10 (DBL) | Protactile movement (Clark; Nuccio & granda) has articulated a DBL-specific spatial philosophy (distantism critique; co-presence design) that has not been translated into architectural specification language anywhere in the reviewed literature. The guidebook is at the frontier. Consider adding a Protactile/co-presence paragraph to §2.10 and relevant Volume 2 items as Co-1 evidence layer. | 2026-03-19 14:30

GAP-STEP5-01 | P1 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | B-10 | SAFETY: Seizure risk disclosure drafted. Jordan & Vanderheiden (2024, PMC11872230) confirms PSE risk at composite flash rates ≥5 Hz from unsynchronised VADs. BS EN 54-23 synchronisation requirement + NFPA 72 ≤2 Hz + supplementary vibrotactile/voice alarm recommended. Add NEU (epilepsy) and NDV/AUT to B-10. Specification language ready for item-specification-writer. | 2026-03-26 05:30

ANSI/ASA S12.60-2010/Part 1 §5.3.2. Specification language ready for item-specification-writer. | 2026-03-26 05:30
GAP-SRS-01 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | A-16 | H-02 (Individual Environmental Control) elevated to PRIMARY cross-reference. Unwin, Powell & Jones (2022, PMID: 34693744, PMC9340127) — user control is the most important design variable for MSE with autistic users. Replicated 2021 + 2024. Location spec added: adjacent to accessible WC. Specification language ready. | 2026-03-26 05:30
GAP-034 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | B-01 | Circadian lighting specification upgraded. Brown et al. (2022, PMC8929548) international consensus: melanopic EDI ≥250 lux daytime; ≤10 lux evening; <1 lux sleep. Guidebook's ≥150 EML was below consensus. Upgraded to ≥250 melanopic EDI (≈276 EML). DIN/TS 67600:2022 and WELL v6 align. Metric equivalence note added. | 2026-03-26 05:30

GAP-059 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | CHD-10 + Supp Vol §I | Rajotte et al. (2024) confirmed: nine-feature neuroinclusive school model, PEO-based, OT-led (Université Laval). Published: J. Occupational Therapy, Schools, & Early Intervention, 18(2), 281–299. DOI: 10.1080/19411243.2024.2341643. Features include: free from excessive stimuli, predictable and structured, clearly defined spaces, appropriate visual support, harnessing passions/strengths, variety of options. Tier 1 (OT design-based research, pilot-tested). Add to CHD-BT-02 and CHD-10 evidence base. | 2026-03-26 06:00
GAP-060 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | A-16 + CHD-10 | NCSE Ireland (2025) Sensory Spaces in Schools confirmed. OT-authored, published March 2025 by NCSE OTs. References Unwin et al. (2022), Rajotte et al. (2024), Dunn sensory processing model. Covers: proactive regulation strategies, sensory room design, classroom sensory screening. Tier 2/3 (OT practice guidance, IE jurisdiction). Add to bibliography Section D and A-16 evidence base. Companion document: NCSE (2024) Physical Classroom Environment. | 2026-03-26 06:00
GAP-063 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | G-03/G-04 + Supp Vol J-03 | Guay et al. (2020) confirmed. Clinical Biomechanics, 80, 105109. DOI: 10.1016/j.clinbiomech.2020.105109. PMID: 32771808. Université de Sherbrooke / Research Centre on Aging. Tier 1 OT biomechanical study. Key finding: grab bars must sustain ≥23.2% body weight + safety factor 1.5. Vertical grab bar most effective for balance. Slippery surfaces increase force duration. This is the closest available Tier 1 evidence for grab bar load specification. Also note: Greene, Levine, Guay & Novak (2023) CJOT 91(2):183-193 — wall-mounted vs rim-mounted biomechanics; Levine et al. (2025) JMIR Rehabil Assist Technol 12:e69442 — grasp location biomechanics. Growing OT biomechanics evidence cluster from Novak/Guay labs. | 2026-03-26 06:00
GAP-RAP-03 | P2 | RESOLVED | multilingual-research | A-08 | NC-25 to dB(A) equivalence confirmed. NC-25 ≈ 30–33 dB(A) depending on spectral content. ANSI S12.60 specifies 35 dBA for classrooms (≈NC-25 to NC-30). NC is a spectral criterion (8 octave bands); dB(A) is broadband — not interchangeable. DIN 18041:2016 uses dB(A), not NC. Cross-reference note drafted: "NC-25 ≈ 30–33 dB(A); ANSI S12.60 35 dBA ≈ NC-25 to NC-30. Where international standards use different noise metrics, this equivalence applies." Add to A-08 evidence basis note. | 2026-03-26 06:00
